'''Stephen John Molloy''' (born 11 March 1969) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Featherstone Rovers]] ([[Captain (sports)|captain]]), [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] (two spells), [[Huddersfield Giants#1999-2000: Huddersfield-Sheffield Giants|Huddersfield]]-[[Sheffield Eagles#Huddersfield–Sheffield Giants|Sheffield Giants]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Batley Bulldogs]], as a {{rlp|pr}},<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and coached at club level for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Steve is the current Head Coach of the ambitious Manchester Rangers RLFC who are currently working on plans to enter the semi-professional & professional ranks in the near future.

==Background==
Steve Molloy was born in Gorton, Manchester, Lancashire, England.<ref name="birthplace">{{cite web |title=Steve Molloy Profile |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/steve-molloy/summary.html |access-date=13 June 2025 |website=Rugby League Project}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Molloy made his début for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on Sunday 28 August 1988, and he played his last match for Warrington on Monday 16 April 1990. He played at {{rlp|pr}} in Warrington's 24–16 victory over [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[1989 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season|1989–90 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Saturday 14 October 1989.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

In 1993, Molloy transferred from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] to [[Featherstone Rovers]]. The transfer fee was decided by a tribunal, with Featherstone paying Leeds an initial fee of £95,000, and an additional £5,000 when he received another cap for Great Britain.<ref>{{cite web |title=Sporting Digest: Rugby League |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/sporting-digest-rugby-league-1463154.html |website=The Independent |access-date=17 August 2023 |date=23 August 1993}}</ref> He made his début for Featherstone Rovers on Sunday 29 August 1993, and he played his last match for the club in September 1997.<ref>{{cite web |title=Molloy, Steve |url=https://www.fevarchive.co.uk/players/steve-molloy/703/ |website=fevarchive.co.uk |access-date=17 August 2023}}</ref>

===International honours===
Steve Molloy won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Leeds in 1992 against Wales, while at Featherstone Rovers in 1996 against France ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and Wales, while at Sheffield Eagles in 1999 against France (2 matches), and won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Leeds in 1993 against France, while at Featherstone Rovers in 1994 against Fiji, 1996 against Fiji ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and New Zealand ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]).

==Coaching career==
In 2002, he became [[player-coach]] at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Molloy takes charge of Oldham |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/2087720.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 August 2023 |date=3 July 2002}}</ref> He left the club at the end of the 2004 season when his contract was not renewed.<ref>{{cite web |title=Molloy makes Oldham exit |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/3698326.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 August 2023 |date=28 September 2004}}</ref>

He spent three seasons coaching Oldham's [[rugby union]] team before accepting a development role with the [[Rugby Football League]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |title=Molloy steps down |url=https://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/13/local-sport-news/15459/molloy-steps-down |website=Oldham Chronicle |access-date=17 August 2023 |date=21 October 2008}}</ref>
